ogTitle: Bonnells Bay vs Bonny Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bonnells Bay and Bonny Hills.
heading: Bonnells Bay vs Bonny Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bonnells Bay, New South Wales and Bonny Hills, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bonnells Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Bonny Hills (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bonnells Bay or Bonny Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bonnells Bay has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Bonny Hills scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bonnells Bay scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Bonny Hills scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bonnells Bay or Bonny Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bonnells Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
